translations.literal: Only the earth below is pitch-dark, endlessly without dawn; to wrap me again in winding burial silks and nail the lid tight brings me no joy.
translations.idiomatic: The grave is endless night; swaddling my corpse in silk and sealing the coffin with tight nails gives me no cheer.

sentences.zh: 幸有前窀，朝殞夕下，措屍靈默，幅巾而已。

translations.literal: Fortunately there is prior burial vault; morning death evening lowered; place corpse in silent spirit hall, headcloth only.
translations.idiomatic: Luckily an old family tomb awaits—bury me at dusk of the day I die, lay me in silence, nothing but a plain cloth about my head.

sentences.zh: 奢非晉文，[63]儉非王孫，[64]推情從意，庶無咎吝。

translations.literal: Extravagance is not Duke Wen of Jin, [63] frugality is not Wangsun; [64] push feeling follow intent, perhaps without blame and stinginess.
translations.idiomatic: I ask neither Duke Wen’s splendor [63] nor Wangsun’s stinginess; [64] let affection decide—and spare me posthumous blame.

sentences.zh: 」諸子從之。

translations.literal: " His sons followed it.
translations.idiomatic: His sons obeyed.

sentences.zh: 武威多為立祠，世世不絕。

translations.literal: Wuwei mostly for him erected shrines; generation after generation unbroken.
translations.idiomatic: Wuwei raised shrines to him that flourished for generations.

sentences.zh: 所著銘、頌、書、教、誡述、志、對策、章表二十四篇。

translations.literal: What he authored—ming, song, letters, instruction, admonition narratives, records, examination replies, memorials—twenty-four pieces.
translations.idiomatic: His writings—inscriptions, hymns, letters, moral essays, admonitions, treatises, examination papers, and memorials—number twenty-four pieces.

sentences.zh: 長子芝，字伯英，最知名。

translations.literal: Eldest son Zhi, courtesy name Boying, most renowned.
translations.idiomatic: His eldest son Zhang Zhi, courtesy Boying, became the most celebrated.

sentences.zh: [65]芝及弟昶，字文舒，並善草書，至今稱傳之。

translations.literal: [65] Zhi and younger brother Chang, courtesy name Wenshu, both good at cursive script—to this day praised and transmitted.
translations.idiomatic: [65] Zhang Zhi and his brother Chang, courtesy Wenshu, mastered cursive hand—masters still speak their names.

sentences.zh: 初，奐為武威太守，其妻懷孕，夢帶奐印綬登樓而歌。

translations.literal: At first when Huan was Wuwei governor, his wife was pregnant, dreamed wearing Huan’s seal-cord ascending tower and singing.
translations.idiomatic: While Huan governed Wuwei his pregnant wife dreamed she wore his official ribbons, climbed a tower, and sang.

sentences.zh: 訊之占者，曰：「必將生男，復臨茲邦，命終此數。

translations.literal: Asked the diviner; said: "Certainly will bear a son, again oversee this state, fate ends at this number."
translations.idiomatic: The diviner said: "You will bear a son who will rule this commandery again—and die when fate counts its span."

sentences.zh: 」既而生子猛，以建安中為武威太守，殺刺史邯鄲商，州兵圍之急，猛恥見擒，乃登樓自燒而死，卒如占云。

translations.literal: " Thereupon bore son Meng; in Jian’an period became Wuwei governor, killed Inspector Handan Shang; provincial troops besieged him urgently; Meng ashamed to be captured, then ascended tower and burned himself to death—finally as divination said.
translations.idiomatic: She bore Zhang Meng, who in the Jian’an era governed Wuwei, murdered Inspector Handan Shang, and—when provincial troops closed in—chose to burn himself atop a tower rather than surrender, fulfilling the omen.

sentences.zh: 論曰：自鄛鄉之封，中官世盛，[66]暴恣數十年閒，四海之內，莫不切齒憤盈，願投兵於其族。

translations.literal: The appraisal states: From Chaoxiang’s enfeoffment, palace officials flourished generation after generation, [66] violent and indulgent several tens of years within; within the four seas none did not gnash teeth with overflowing rage, wishing to cast weapons on their clans.
translations.idiomatic: Since the great houses enfeoffed the eunuchs, [66] they tyrannized the realm for decades until every corner gnashed its teeth and hungered to destroy their kin.

sentences.zh: 陳蕃、竇武奮義草謀，征會天下，名士有識所共聞也，而張奐見欺豎子，揚戈以斷忠烈。

translations.literal: Chen Fan, Dou Wu roused righteousness drafting plans, summoned meeting the realm—famous scholars with insight jointly heard it—yet Zhang Huan was deceived by boys, raised spear to cut off loyal and fierce.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Fan and Dou Wu summoned the realm to righteous arms—every scholar knew—yet Zhang Huan let palace striplings trick him into raising spears against men loyal unto death.

sentences.zh: [67]雖恨毒在心，辭爵謝咎。

translations.literal: [67] Although hatred poison in heart, declined noble rank and apologized for fault.
translations.idiomatic: [67] Though bitterness gnawed him, he renounced honors and owned his wrong.

sentences.zh: 詩云：「啜其泣矣，何嗟及矣！

translations.literal: Odes says: "Sipping they weep—alas how late to reach!"
translations.idiomatic: The Classic of Poetry says: "They sob into their sleeves—what good is regret now?"

translations.literal: Closing quotation from the Classic of Poetry.
translations.idiomatic: Here ends the quotation from the ode.

sentences.zh: 段熲字紀明，武威姑臧人也。

translations.literal: Duan Jiong, courtesy name Jiming, was a man of Guzang in Wuwei.
translations.idiomatic: Duan Jiong, courtesy Jiming, came from Guzang in Wuwei commandery.

sentences.zh: 其先出鄭共叔段，西域都護會宗之從曾孫也。

translations.literal: His ancestors came from Duke Duan of Gong at Zheng; he was Western Regions protector Huizong’s collateral great-grandson.
translations.idiomatic: His line traced to Duke Duan of Zheng; he was a collateral descendant of Gan Huizong, protector-general of the Western Regions.

sentences.zh: [69]熲少便習弓馬，尚遊俠，輕財賄，長乃折節好古學。

translations.literal: [69] Jiong in youth was practiced at bow and horse, esteemed wandering knights, slighted wealth and bribes; grown he bent his disposition and loved ancient learning.
translations.idiomatic: [69] As a youth he excelled at riding and archery, admired roaming swordsmen, and scorned riches; with age he turned scholar and cultivated classical studies.

sentences.zh: 初舉孝廉，為憲陵園丞、陽陵令，[70]所在〔有〕能政。

translations.literal: First recommended filial and incorrupt; became Xianling park assistant, Yangling magistrate, [70] wherever he served his governance was capable.
translations.idiomatic: Recommended as filial and incorrupt, he served as steward of Emperor Shun’s mausoleum park and magistrate of Yangling; [70] every post saw sound administration.

sentences.zh: 遷遼東屬國都尉。

translations.literal: Transferred Dependent State Commandant of Liaodong.
translations.idiomatic: He was promoted commandant of the dependent state in Liaodong.

sentences.zh: 時鮮卑犯塞，熲即率所領馳赴之。

translations.literal: At that time Xianbei violated the passes; Jiong immediately led those under his command galloping to them.
translations.idiomatic: When the Xianbei breached the frontier he dashed to meet them with every man under his command.

sentences.zh: 既而恐賊驚去，乃使驛騎詐繼璽書詔熲，熲於道偽退，潛於還路設伏。

translations.literal: Soon fearing bandits startled and fled, had relay riders forge a follow-on sealed edict recalling Jiong; Jiong on the road feigned retreat, secretly on the return road set ambush.
translations.idiomatic: Fearing they would scatter if pressed, he had dispatch riders fake a recall order; he pretended to withdraw, then lay in ambush along their retreat.

sentences.zh: 虜以為信然，乃入追熲。

translations.literal: The barbarians thought it true, then entered pursuing Jiong.
translations.idiomatic: The tribesmen swallowed the ruse and chased him.

sentences.zh: 熲因大縱兵，悉斬獲之。

translations.literal: Jiong thereupon greatly unleashed troops; all were beheaded or captured.
translations.idiomatic: Jiong wheeled and destroyed them to the last man.

sentences.zh: 坐詐璽書伏重刑，以有功論司寇。

translations.literal: Sat for forging sealed edict subject to heavy punishment; because of merit sentenced minister-of-crime labor.
translations.idiomatic: Forging an imperial rescript earned him a capital sentence; merit commuted it to minister-of-crime labor.

sentences.zh: 刑竟，徵拜議郎。

translations.literal: When punishment ended he was summoned as gentleman consultant.
translations.idiomatic: When his sentence ended the court named him gentleman consultant.

sentences.zh: 時太山、琅邪賊東郭竇、公孫舉等聚觿三萬人，破壞郡縣，遣兵討之，連年不克。

translations.literal: At that time Mount Tai, Langye bandits Dongguo Dou, Gongsun Ju and others gathered armies thirty thousand persons, destroyed commanderies and counties; troops sent to punish them, successive years could not overcome.
translations.idiomatic: Dongguo Dou and Gongsun Ju had raised thirty thousand bandits in Taishan and Langye, wrecking local government; imperial columns spent years unable to crush them.

sentences.zh: 永壽二年，桓帝詔公卿選將有文武者，司徒尹*

translations.literal: Second year of Yongshou, Emperor Huan ordered dukes and ministers select generals possessing civil and military ability; Minister of Education Yin—
translations.idiomatic: In Yongshou 2 (156 CE) Emperor Huan ordered the high ministers to nominate a commander versed in both letters and arms; Minister of Education Yin—

translations.literal: (litigation)
translations.idiomatic: (scribal gloss in source)

sentences.zh: **〔頌〕*薦熲，[71]乃拜為中郎將。

translations.literal: **〔Song〕* recommended Jiong, [71] then appointed general of the household.
translations.idiomatic: —Yin Song recommended Jiong, [71] who was commissioned general of the household.

sentences.zh: 擊竇、舉等，大破斬之，獲首萬餘級，餘黨降散。

translations.literal: Attacked Dou, Ju and others, greatly defeated and beheaded them, obtained heads more than ten thousand ranks; remainder factions surrendered and scattered.
translations.idiomatic: He smashed Dou and Ju, took over ten thousand heads, and broke their remnant bands.

sentences.zh: 封熲為列侯，賜錢五十萬，除一子為郎中。

translations.literal: Enfeoffed Jiong full marquis, gifted cash five hundred thousand, appointed one son gentleman consultant.
translations.idiomatic: The throne made him a full marquis, granted five hundred thousand cash, and gave one son a gentleman consultant’s berth.

sentences.zh: 延熹二年，遷護羌校尉。

translations.literal: Second year of Yanxi, transferred Colonel Protector of the Qiang.
translations.idiomatic: In Yanxi 2 (159 CE) he became Colonel Protector of the Qiang.

sentences.zh: 會燒當、燒何、當煎、勒姐等八種羌[72]寇隴西、金城塞，熲將兵及湟中義從羌萬二千騎出湟谷，擊破之。

translations.literal: It happened Shaodang, Shaohe, Dangjian, Lejie and other eight kinds of Qiang [72] raided Longxi, Jincheng barrier; Jiong led troops and Huangzhong volunteer-follower Qiang twelve thousand cavalry out Huang valley, defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Eight Qiang confederations—Shaodang, Shaohe, Dangjian, Lejie, and allies [72]—struck Longxi and Jincheng; Jiong marched twelve thousand Han and loyal Qiang horse out of the Huangzhong gorge and broke them.

sentences.zh: 追討南度河，使軍吏田晏、夏育募先登，懸索相引，復戰於羅亭，大破之，斬其酋豪以下二千級，獲生口萬餘人，虜皆奔走。

translations.literal: Pursued attacking south crossing river; ordered army clerks Tian Yan, Xia Yu recruit first ascenders; hung ropes mutually pulling; again battled at Luoting, greatly defeated them, beheaded their chieftains below two thousand ranks, captured living mouths more than ten thousand persons; barbarians all fled.
translations.idiomatic: He chased them south across the river, sent officers Tian Yan and Xia Yu to lead shock troops up cliff ropes, and fought again at Luoting. Two thousand chiefs fell, ten thousand captives were taken, and the enemy fled headlong.

sentences.zh: 明年春，余羌復與燒何大豪寇張掖，攻沒鉅鹿塢，殺屬國吏民，又招同種千餘落，並兵晨奔熲軍。

translations.literal: Next spring remaining Qiang again with Shaohe great chief raided Zhangye, attacked and drowned Julu fort, killed dependent state clerks and people; again summoned same kind more than one thousand settlements, joined troops morning rushing Jiong’s army.
translations.idiomatic: The next spring stray Qiang joined Shaohe headmen to strike Zhangye, overwhelm Julu stockade, slaughter dependent-state households, rally another thousand settlements, and charge Jiong’s camp at dawn.

sentences.zh: 熲下馬大戰，至日中，刀折矢盡，虜亦引退。

translations.literal: Jiong dismounted great battle; reached midday, swords broke arrows exhausted; barbarians also drew back and withdrew.
translations.idiomatic: Jiong fought on foot until noon broke every blade and emptied every quiver; the enemy then peeled away.

sentences.zh: 熲追之，且□且行，晝夜相攻，割肉食雪，四十餘日，遂至河首積石山，出塞二千餘里，斬燒何大帥，首虜五千餘人。

translations.literal: Jiong pursued them, halting and marching by turns (text damaged:), day and night attacking each other, cutting flesh and eating snow; after more than forty days he reached Jishi Mountain at the river’s source, more than two thousand li beyond the frontier passes, beheaded the Shaohe commander-in-chief, and counted more than five thousand heads or captives.
translations.idiomatic: He harried them for forty days through snow and starvation strikes, reaching Mount Jishi at the river’s source beyond two thousand li of steppe; the Shaohe commander fell with five thousand counted dead or taken.

sentences.zh: 又分兵擊石城羌，斬首溺死者千六百人。

translations.literal: Again divided troops attacking Stone Fort Qiang, beheaded and drowned dead one thousand six hundred persons.
translations.idiomatic: A detached column hit the Shicheng Qiang and left sixteen hundred killed or drowned.

sentences.zh: 燒當種九十餘口詣熲降。

translations.literal: Shaodang kind more than ninety mouths went to Jiong to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: More than ninety Shaodang clansmen came in to Jiong.

sentences.zh: 又雜種羌屯聚白石，[73]熲復進擊，首虜三千餘人。

translations.literal: Again mixed-kind Qiang gathered at Baishi; [73] Jiong again advanced attacking, heads and captives more than three thousand persons.
translations.idiomatic: Mixed Qiang bands rallied at Baishi; [73] Jiong struck again and counted three thousand heads or prisoners.

sentences.zh: 冬，勒姐、零吾種圍允街，[74]殺略吏民，熲排營救之，斬獲數百人。

translations.literal: Winter, Lejie, Lingwu kinds besieged Yunjie, [74] killed and seized clerks and people; Jiong pushed camps to rescue them, beheaded and captured several hundred persons.
translations.idiomatic: That winter Lejie and Lingwu warriors besieged Yunjie [74] and butchered the town until Jiong thrust forward his camps and killed hundreds in the relief.

sentences.zh: 四年冬，上郡沉氐、隴西牢姐、烏吾諸種羌共寇並涼二州，熲將湟中義從討之。

translations.literal: Fourth year winter, Shangjun Chenshi, Longxi Laojie, Wuwu various-kind Qiang jointly raided Bing and Liang two prefectures; Jiong led Huangzhong volunteer-followers to punish them.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year of Yanxi, Chenshi bands from Shangjun joined Laojie and Wuwu clans from Longxi to ravage Bing and Liang; Jiong marched loyal Huangzhong auxiliaries against them.

sentences.zh: 涼州刺史郭閎貪共其功，稽固熲軍，使不得進。

translations.literal: Liangzhou Inspector Guo Hong greedy to share his merit, delayed and blocked Jiong’s army, caused inability to advance.
translations.idiomatic: Inspector Guo Hong of Liangzhou, hungry to share the glory, stalled Jiong’s advance.

sentences.zh: [75]義從役久，戀鄉舊，皆悉反叛。

translations.literal: [75] Volunteer-followers served long, longed for native villages—all rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: [75] The auxiliaries, weary and homesick, rose in mutiny.

sentences.zh: 郭閎歸罪於熲，熲坐征下獄，輸作左校。

translations.literal: Guo Hong shifted blame onto Jiong; Jiong sat summoned down to prison, sentenced to labor Left Workshops.
translations.idiomatic: Guo Hong pinned the revolt on Jiong; an edict recalled him to jail and the Left Workshops.

sentences.zh: 羌遂陸梁，覆沒營塢，轉相招結，唐突諸郡，於是吏人守闕訟熲以千數。

translations.literal: Qiang then rampaged, overturned camps and forts, turned summoning one another, crashed against various commanderies; then clerks and people guarded palace gate suing for Jiong by thousands.
translations.idiomatic: The Qiang ran wild, stormed stockades, linked clan to clan, and trampled command after commandery—until thousands of officials and commoners besieged the palace gates demanding Jiong’s reinstatement.

sentences.zh: 朝廷知熲為郭閎所誣，詔問其狀。

translations.literal: The court knew Jiong was framed by Guo Hong; edict inquired into the situation.
translations.idiomatic: The court saw through Guo Hong’s frame and ordered an inquiry.

sentences.zh: 熲但謝罪，不敢言枉，京師稱為長者。

translations.literal: Jiong only apologized for crime, dared not speak wronged; capital called him elder gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: Jiong apologized without crying injustice; Luoyang hailed him as a man of honor.

sentences.zh: 起於徒中，復拜議郎，遷并州刺史。

translations.literal: Raised from convict ranks, again appointed gentleman consultant, transferred Bingzhou inspector.
translations.idiomatic: Raised from penal labor, he became gentleman consultant and then inspector of Bingzhou.

sentences.zh: 時滇那等諸種羌五六千人寇武威、張掖、酒泉，燒人廬舍。

translations.literal: At that time Dianna and other various-kind Qiang five or six thousand raided Wuwei, Zhangye, Jiuquan, burned people’s huts.
translations.idiomatic: Some five or six thousand Qiang under Dianna and allied headmen swept Wuwei, Zhangye, and Jiuquan, putting homesteads to the torch.

sentences.zh: 六年，寇埶轉盛，涼州幾亡。

translations.literal: Sixth year, raider momentum turned strong; Liangzhou nearly perished.
translations.idiomatic: By the sixth year of Yanxi the rebels had nearly swallowed Liangzhou.

sentences.zh: 冬，復以熲為護羌校尉，乘驛之職。

translations.literal: Winter, again made Jiong Colonel Protector of the Qiang, riding relay to office.
translations.idiomatic: That winter he was reappointed colonel and raced by post-chaise to headquarters.

sentences.zh: 明年春，羌封僇、良多、滇那等[76]酋豪三百五十五人率三千落詣熲降。

translations.literal: Next spring Qiang Fenglu, Liangduo, Dianna and others [76] chieftains three hundred fifty-five persons led three thousand settlements went to Jiong to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: The next spring three hundred fifty-five chiefs—Fenglu, Liangduo, Dianna, and others [76]—brought three thousand settlements to surrender at Jiong’s camp.

sentences.zh: 當煎、勒姐種猶自屯結。

translations.literal: Dangjian, Lejie kinds still themselves gathered and linked.
translations.idiomatic: Dangjian and Lejie bands still held out in fortified camps.

sentences.zh: 冬，熲將萬餘人擊破之，斬其酋豪，首虜四千餘人。

translations.literal: Winter, Jiong led more than ten thousand defeated them, beheaded their chieftains, heads and captives more than four thousand persons.
translations.idiomatic: That winter he threw ten thousand men against them, slew their headmen, and counted four thousand dead or captive.

sentences.zh: 八年春，熲復擊勒姐種，斬首四百餘級，降者二千餘人。

translations.literal: Eighth year spring, Jiong again attacked Lejie kind, beheaded more than four hundred ranks; surrendered more than two thousand persons.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of Yanxi 8 he struck the Lejie again—four hundred heads and two thousand surrendering warriors.

sentences.zh: 夏，進軍擊當煎種於湟中，熲兵敗，被圍三日，用隱士樊志張策，潛師夜出，鳴鼓還戰，大破之，首虜數千人。

translations.literal: Summer, advanced army attacking Dangjian kind in Huangzhong; Jiong’s troops defeated, surrounded three days; used recluse Fan Zhizhang’s plan, secretly troops night exited, beat drums returning battle, greatly defeated them, heads and captives several thousand persons.
translations.idiomatic: That summer his assault on the Dangjian in Huangzhong went wrong—three days besieged until recluse Fan Zhizhang’s stratagem slipped the army out by night; drums thundered at dawn and thousands of tribesmen fell.

sentences.zh: 熲遂窮追，展轉山谷閒，自春及秋，無日不戰，虜遂饑困敗散，北略武威閒。

translations.literal: Jiong thereupon pursued exhaustively, shifting among valleys and mountains; from spring through autumn no day without battle; barbarians then hungry exhausted defeated scattered, north raiding Wuwei region.
translations.idiomatic: He dogged them ridge to ravine from spring to autumn without a day’s pause until hunger broke them; survivors fled north to raid toward Wuwei.

sentences.zh: 熲凡破西羌，斬首二萬三千級，獲生口數萬人，馬牛羊八百萬頭，降者萬餘落。

translations.literal: Jiong in all defeated western Qiang, beheaded twenty-three thousand ranks, captured living mouths several tens of thousands, horses cattle sheep eight million head, surrendered more than ten thousand settlements.
translations.idiomatic: In sum his western campaigns took twenty-three thousand heads, tens of thousands of captives, eight million head of livestock, and brought more than ten thousand settlements to surrender.

sentences.zh: 封熲都鄉侯，邑五百戶。

translations.literal: Enfeoffed Jiong village marquis of Dou, fief five hundred households.
translations.idiomatic: He was enfeoffed village marquis of Douxiang with five hundred households.

sentences.zh: 永康元年，當煎諸種復反，合四千餘人，欲攻武威，熲復追擊於鸞鳥，大破之，[77]殺其渠帥，斬首三千餘級，西羌於此弭定。

translations.literal: First year of Yongkang, Dangjian various kinds again rebelled, combined more than four thousand persons, wished to attack Wuwei; Jiong again pursued attacked at Luanniao, greatly defeated them, [77] killed their channel leaders, beheaded more than three thousand ranks; western Qiang hereupon were pacified and settled.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongkang 1 (167 CE) four thousand Dangjian warriors marched on Wuwei; Jiong ran them down at Luanniao, slew their chiefs, took three thousand heads, [77] and the western Qiang were finally quiet.

sentences.zh: 而東羌先零等，自覆沒征西將軍馬賢後，朝廷不能討，遂數寇擾三輔。

translations.literal: But eastern Qiang Xianling and others, since overturning and drowning General Who Conquers the West Ma Xian, the court could not punish; thereupon repeatedly raided and disturbed Three Adjuncts.
translations.idiomatic: East of the passes the Xianling and their allies, unbeaten since Ma Xian’s disaster, still plagued the Three Adjuncts while the court hesitated.

sentences.zh: 其後度遼將軍皇甫規、中郎將張奐招之連年，既降又叛。

translations.literal: Afterward General Who Crosses the Liao Huangfu Gui and General of the Household Zhang Huan summoned them year after year; they surrendered and rebelled again.
translations.idiomatic: Huangfu Gui and Zhang Huan spent years coaxing them in—each time they submitted they rose again.

sentences.zh: 桓帝詔問熲曰：「先零東羌造惡反逆，而皇甫規、張奐各擁強觿，不時輯定。

translations.literal: Emperor Huan issued an edict asking Jiong: "The Xianling eastern Qiang have wrought evil and rebellion, yet Huangfu Gui and Zhang Huan each hold strong armies and have not pacified them in good time.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Huan asked Jiong: The eastern Xianling Qiang remain rebels, while Huangfu Gui and Zhang Huan sit on large forces yet delay pacification.

sentences.zh: 欲熲移兵東討，未識其宜，可參思術略。

translations.literal: We wish you to shift troops east to punish them; we do not yet know whether that is fitting—ponder tactics and submit advice.
translations.idiomatic: Should we move your army east? Weigh the strategy and advise us.

sentences.zh: 」熲因上言曰：「臣伏見先零東羌雖數叛逆，而降於皇甫規者，已二萬許落，善惡既分，餘寇無幾。

translations.literal: " Jiong therefore submitted: "Your servant has observed that although the Xianling eastern Qiang have rebelled repeatedly, those who surrendered to Huangfu Gui already number about twenty thousand settlements; good and evil are sorted and few raiders remain.
translations.idiomatic: Jiong replied: The eastern Xianling have risen often, yet some twenty thousand settlements already yielded to Huangfu Gui—loyal and hostile are largely sorted; the remnant is small.

sentences.zh: 今張奐躊躇久不進者，當慮外離內合，兵往必驚。

translations.literal: Now the reason Zhang Huan hesitates and long does not advance must be that he fears outward division yet inward union—if troops go they will surely be startled.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Huan tarries because he dreads driving allies apart only to see them reunite; any march would stampede them.

sentences.zh: 且自冬踐春，屯結不散，人畜疲羸，自亡之埶，徒更招降，坐制強敵耳。

translations.literal: Moreover from winter stepping into spring they camp linked and do not disperse; men and beasts are weary and wasted—a momentum of self-destruction; only further summon surrender is needed to sit and control a strong foe.
translations.idiomatic: They have huddled in camp since winter into spring, men and livestock dropping from exhaustion—they are rotting on their own. Keep offering surrender and you pin the enemy without a blow.

sentences.zh: 臣以為狼子野心，難以恩納，[78]埶窮雖服，兵去復動。

translations.literal: Your servant considers them wolf cubs with wild hearts—hard to win with kindness; [78] though they submit when pressed, they stir again once spears leave.
translations.idiomatic: They are wolfish by nature—kindness cannot hold them; [78] corner them and they yield; lift the army and they revolt.

sentences.zh: 唯當長矛挾脅，白刃加頸耳。

translations.literal: Only long spears at the ribs and bright blades on the neck will do.
translations.idiomatic: Only steel at the throat will answer.

sentences.zh: 計東種所餘三萬餘落，居近塞內，路無險折，非有燕、齊、秦、趙從橫之埶，而久亂並、涼，累侵三輔，西河、上郡，已各內徙，安定、北地，復至單危，自雲中、五原，西至漢陽二千餘里，匈奴、種羌，並□其地，是為漢疽伏疾，留滯脅下，如不加誅，轉就滋大。

translations.literal: In reckoning, the eastern tribes still number more than thirty thousand settlements, dwelling close inside the passes where roads hold no sharp defiles and they lack the checkerboard depth of Yan, Qi, Qin, or Zhao—yet long they have ravaged Bing and Liang, repeatedly struck the Three Adjuncts; Xihe and Shangjun were moved inward, Anding and Beidi stand solitary and perilous; from Yunzhong and Wuyuan west two thousand li to Hanyang, Xiongnu and mixed Qiang together occupy the land—this is Han’s abscess and latent sore lodged under the ribs; if not cut out it will swell.
translations.idiomatic: Some thirty thousand eastern settlements hug the inner frontier—no mountain maze like old central-plain states—yet they have gutted Bing and Liang for years, hammered the Three Adjuncts, forced Xihe and Shangjun to relocate, left Anding and Beidi exposed, and from Yunzhong–Wuyuan westward two thousand li to Hanyang Xiongnu and Qiang share the ground—a festering boil on the empire’s flank that must be lanced or it grows.

sentences.zh: 今若以騎五千，步萬人，車三千兩，三冬二夏，足以破定，無慮用費為錢五十四億。

translations.literal: Now if with five thousand horse, ten thousand foot, and three thousand baggage carts, through three winters and two summers, it is enough to break and settle them—estimated expense no more than five billion four hundred million cash.
translations.idiomatic: Five thousand cavalry, ten thousand infantry, three thousand supply carts, and two full cycles of seasons—fifty-four hundred million cash—would finish them.

sentences.zh: [79]如此，則可令腢羌破盡，匈奴長服，內徙郡縣，得反本土。

translations.literal: [79] Thus you could break every Qiang faction, keep the Xiongnu long submissive, and let relocated interior commanderies return home.
translations.idiomatic: [79] Then the Qiang could be crushed outright, the Xiongnu cowed, and resettled interior counties moved back to their old soil.

sentences.zh: 伏計永初中，諸羌反叛，十有四年，用二百四十億；

translations.literal: Your servant recalls that in the Yongchu era the various Qiang rebelled fourteen years and cost twenty-four billion.
translations.idiomatic: The Yongchu rebellions ran fourteen years and burned twenty-four billion.

sentences.zh: 永和之末，復經七年，用八十餘億。

translations.literal: At the close of Yonghe another seven years consumed more than eight billion.
translations.idiomatic: Yonghe’s closing campaigns took seven years and over eight billion more.

sentences.zh: 費耗若此，猶不誅盡，餘孽復起，於茲作害。

translations.literal: Costs such as these still did not exterminate them; remaining spawn rose again and here harm us anew.
translations.idiomatic: Even that outlay left vermin alive to rise again and wound us.

sentences.zh: 今不暫疲人，則永寧無期。

translations.literal: If we do not weary the people briefly, lasting peace will never come.
translations.idiomatic: Unless the realm accepts short pain now, quiet never comes.

sentences.zh: 臣庶竭駑劣，伏待節度。

translations.literal: Your servant will exhaust his sorry talent and await orders.
translations.idiomatic: I beg leave to spend my mean strength as you direct.

sentences.zh: 」帝許之，悉聽如所上。

translations.literal: " The emperor approved and granted all he proposed.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor approved every item.

sentences.zh: 建寧元年春，熲將兵萬餘人，繼十五日糧，從彭陽直指高平，[80]與先零諸種戰於逢義山。

translations.literal: Spring of the first year of Jianning, Jiong led more than ten thousand troops with fifteen days’ grain, from Pengyang straight to Gaoping, [80] and fought the Xianling confederation at Fengyi Mountain.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianning 1 (168 CE) spring he marched ten thousand men with a fortnight’s supplies from Pengyang toward Gaoping [80] and met the Xianling at Fengyi Mountain.

sentences.zh: 虜兵盛，熲觿恐。

translations.literal: The enemy host was massive and Jiong’s army feared.
translations.idiomatic: The tribesmen outnumbered him and his men quailed.

sentences.zh: 熲乃令軍中張鏃利刃，長矛三重，挾以強弩，列輕騎為左右翼。

translations.literal: Jiong then ordered the camp to bare arrowheads and blades, set three ranks of long spears, sandwich them with strong crossbows, and array light horse as wings left and right.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered glittering arms displayed—three spear lines flanked by heavy crossbowmen and light cavalry on either wing.

sentences.zh: 淚怒兵將曰：「今去家數千里，進則事成，走必盡死，努力共功名！

translations.literal: He sternly rebuked officers and men: "We are thousands of li from home—advance and we win fame; flee and we all die—strive together for glory!
translations.idiomatic: He roared at the ranks: "Thousands of li from hearth—push forward or die together—fight for the honor we came for!"

sentences.zh: 」因大呼，觿皆應騰赴，熲馳騎於傍，突而擊之，虜觿大潰，斬首八千餘級，獲牛馬羊二十八萬頭。

translations.literal: " With a great shout the whole army surged; Jiong galloped along the flank, burst in, and routed them—more than eight thousand heads, twenty-eight thousand head of oxen, horses, and sheep.
translations.idiomatic: A thunderous cheer answered; Jiong swept the flank and shattered them—eight thousand heads and twenty-eight thousand livestock.

sentences.zh: 時竇太后臨朝，下詔曰：「先零東羌歷載為患，熲前陳狀，欲必埽滅。

translations.literal: Empress Dowager Dou held court and issued an edict: "The Xianling eastern Qiang have plagued us for generations; Jiong earlier laid out a plan to scour them utterly.
translations.idiomatic: Regent Dou’s edict read: The eastern Xianling have long been a scourge; Colonel Jiong vowed to wipe them out.

sentences.zh: 涉履霜雪，兼行晨夜，身當矢石，感厲吏士。

translations.literal: He trod frost and snow, marched day and night together, himself faced arrows and stones, and inspired clerks and soldiers.
translations.idiomatic: He marched through ice and dark, took bolts himself, and steeled his men.

sentences.zh: 曾未浹日，凶丑奔破，[81]連屍積俘，掠獲無筭。

translations.literal: Before a full cycle of days the villains broke and fled; [81] corpses and captives piled beyond count.
translations.idiomatic: Inside ten days the rebels shattered; [81] dead and captured heaped beyond reckoning.

sentences.zh: 洗雪百年之逋負，以慰忠將之亡魂。

translations.literal: He washed away a century of shame and comforted loyal generals’ wandering souls.
translations.idiomatic: He avenged a hundred years of defeat and gave peace to fallen loyalists.

sentences.zh: [82]功用顯著，朕甚嘉之。

translations.literal: [82] Merit so plain wins my deep praise.
translations.idiomatic: [82] Such service merits my warm approval.

sentences.zh: 須東羌盡定，當並錄功勤。

translations.literal: When the eastern Qiang are wholly settled, merit will be recorded together.
translations.idiomatic: Full honors will follow once the east is quiet.

sentences.zh: 今且賜熲錢二十萬，以家一人為郎中。

translations.literal: For now grant Jiong two hundred thousand cash and make one family member gentleman consultant.
translations.idiomatic: For now award him two hundred thousand cash and a gentleman post for a kinsman.

sentences.zh: 」□中藏府調金錢彩物，增助軍費。

translations.literal: [text damaged: ] The inner treasury was ordered to supply gold, cash, and silks to swell military funds.
translations.idiomatic: The privy purse released bullion, coin, and brocade for the war chest.

sentences.zh: 拜熲羌將軍。

translations.literal: Jiong was appointed general against the Qiang.
translations.idiomatic: He received the title general against the Qiang.

sentences.zh: 夏，熲復追羌出橋門，至走馬水上。

translations.literal: Summer: Jiong again drove the Qiang out Bridge Gate to the Zouma River.
translations.idiomatic: That summer he chased them past Bridge Gate to the Running-Horse River.

sentences.zh: [83]尋聞虜在奢延澤，[84]乃將輕兵兼行，一日一夜二百餘里，晨及賊，擊破之。

translations.literal: [83] Learning they lay at Sheyan Marsh, [84] he took light troops on a forced march—more than two hundred li in a day and night—struck at dawn, and broke them.
translations.idiomatic: [83] Hearing they camped at Sheyan Marsh [84] he raced light infantry two hundred li without halt, hit them at dawn, and routed the lot.

sentences.zh: 余虜走向落川，復相屯結。

translations.literal: Survivors fled toward Luochuan and regrouped.
translations.idiomatic: Survivors bolted for Luochuan and rallied.

sentences.zh: 熲乃分遣騎司馬田晏將五千人出其東，假司馬夏育將二千人繞其西。

translations.literal: Jiong detached Cavalry Major Tian Yan with five thousand men to their east and acting major Xia Yu with two thousand to swing west.
translations.idiomatic: He sent Tian Yan east with five thousand and Xia Yu west with two thousand.

sentences.zh: 羌分六七千人攻圍晏等，晏等與戰，羌潰走。

translations.literal: The Qiang split six or seven thousand to besiege Yan and company; Yan fought them and they broke.
translations.idiomatic: Six or seven thousand Qiang surrounded Tian Yan; he fought free and they scattered.

sentences.zh: 熲急進，與晏等共追之於令鮮水上。

translations.literal: Jiong pressed forward and with Yan caught them on the Linxian River.
translations.idiomatic: Jiong closed up and with Yan cornered them on the Linxian River.

sentences.zh: [85]熲士卒飢渴，乃勒觿推方奪其水，[86]虜復散走。

translations.literal: [85] Jiong’s men were parched; he formed a square, seized the water, [86] and the enemy fled again.
translations.idiomatic: [85] His troops were dying of thirst; he locked shields, seized the ford [86], and the Qiang broke.

sentences.zh: 熲遂與相連綴，且□且引，及於靈武谷。

translations.literal: Jiong stayed on their heels, halting and drawing them on by turns (text damaged:), until he reached Lingwu Valley.
translations.idiomatic: He dogged them in stop-and-go pursuit to Lingwu Gorge.

sentences.zh: [87]熲乃被甲先登，士卒無敢後者。

translations.literal: [87] He donned armor and led the charge—no soldier lagged.
translations.idiomatic: [87] He armored himself and sprang first—none dared hang back.

sentences.zh: 羌遂大敗，□兵而走。

translations.literal: The Qiang were utterly beaten and cast aside arms in flight (text damaged:).
translations.idiomatic: The Qiang collapsed and dropped arms in flight.

sentences.zh: 追之三日三夜，士皆重繭。

translations.literal: He pursued three days and three nights until every foot wore blisters.
translations.idiomatic: Three days and nights of pursuit left every sole raw.

sentences.zh: [88]既到涇陽，[89]餘寇四千落，悉散入漢陽山谷閒。

translations.literal: [88] Reaching Jingyang, [89] four thousand remnant settlements scattered into the Hanyang hills.
translations.idiomatic: [88] At Jingyang [89] four thousand surviving settlements melted into the Hanyang ranges.

sentences.zh: 時張奐上言：「東羌雖破，余種難盡，熲性輕果，慮負敗難常。

translations.literal: Zhang Huan memorialized: "Although the eastern Qiang are broken, remnant kinds are hard to finish; Jiong is rash and bold—I fear defeat cannot be ruled out.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Huan warned: The east is beaten but not erased; Jiong is reckless—disaster may follow.

sentences.zh: 宜且以恩降，可無後悔。

translations.literal: For now win them with kindness and avoid later regret.
translations.idiomatic: Conciliate them now and spare remorse.

sentences.zh: 」詔書下熲。

translations.literal: " The edict was forwarded to Jiong.
translations.idiomatic: The throne sent Zhang’s words to Jiong.

sentences.zh: 熲復上言：「臣本知東羌雖觿，而剁弱易制，所以比陳愚慮，思為永寧之筭。

translations.literal: Jiong answered: "Your servant knew the eastern Qiang were numerous yet fractious and easy to master—hence my repeated counsel for a plan of lasting quiet.
translations.idiomatic: Jiong shot back: I said the eastern tribes were many but splintered—easy prey—and urged a lasting solution.

sentences.zh: 而中郎將張奐，說虜強難破，宜用招降。

translations.literal: General of the Household Zhang Huan claims they are too strong to crush and urges surrender.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Huan calls them invincible and peddles amnesty.

sentences.zh: 聖朝明監，信納瞽言，故臣謀得行，奐計不用。

translations.literal: The sage court saw clearly and trusted my blind words—so my strategy ran and Huan’s did not.
translations.idiomatic: The court believed me, not him.

sentences.zh: 事埶相反，遂懷猜恨。

translations.literal: Positions opposed—therefore he nurses suspicion and spite.
translations.idiomatic: We disagreed—so he nurses a grudge.

sentences.zh: 信叛羌之訴，飾潤辭意，雲臣兵累見折磨，[90]又言羌一氣所生，不可誅盡，[91]山谷廣大，不可空靜，血流污野，傷和致災。

translations.literal: He credits rebel Qiang pleas, polishes their tale, says my troops suffer repeated torture, [90] claims Qiang share one breath and cannot be utterly killed, [91] that valleys are vast and cannot be emptied, that blood fouls the wild and offends harmony inviting calamity.
translations.idiomatic: He repeats rebel propaganda—that my men are worn to the bone, [90] that Qiang are kin one cannot extirpate, [91] that hills are too wide to clear, that slaughter poisons heaven.

sentences.zh: 臣伏念周秦之際，戎狄為害，中興以來，羌寇最盛，誅之不盡，雖降復叛。

translations.literal: Your servant reflects: between Zhou and Qin the Rong and Di harmed China; since the restoration none have ravaged like the Qiang—kill them never to the end, though they surrender they rebel.
translations.idiomatic: From Zhou through Qin barbarians scourged us; since Guangwu none matched the Qiang—never fully killed, always rising again.

sentences.zh: 今先零雜種，累以反覆，攻沒縣邑，剽略人物，發頤露屍，禍及生死，上天震怒，假手行誅。

translations.literal: Now the Xianling mixed bands have turned coat again and again, stormed counties, plundered people, left flesh exposed to sun—harm reaching living and dead; high heaven rages and lends our arms to punish.
translations.idiomatic: Today’s Xianling mix betray every treaty, sack towns, strip corpses—wrath from above uses our blades.

sentences.zh: [92]昔邢為無道，□國伐之，師興而雨。

translations.literal: [92] Anciently wicked Xing drew punishment—hosts marched and rain fell.
translations.idiomatic: [92] When lawless Xing was attacked the army marched and rain blessed them.

sentences.zh: [93]臣動兵涉夏，連獲甘澍，歲時豐稔，人無疵疫。

translations.literal: [93] Your servant campaigned through summer, yet gained sweet rains and rich harvests—no plague among the people.
translations.idiomatic: [93] My summer campaigns brought timely rain and full granaries—no pestilence.

sentences.zh: 上佔天心，不為災傷；

translations.literal: Above matches heaven’s mind—no harm from omens;
translations.idiomatic: Heaven signs favor us—

sentences.zh: [94]下察人事，觿和師克。

translations.literal: [94] below inspects human affairs—the host is harmonious and victorious.
translations.idiomatic: [94] and our army stands united and wins.

sentences.zh: [95]自橋門以西，落川以東，故*

translations.literal: [95] From Bridge Gate west to Luochuan east the former—
translations.idiomatic: [95] From Bridge Gate west to Luochuan east the old—

translations.literal: (palace)
translations.idiomatic: (scribal gloss in source)

sentences.zh: **〔官〕*縣邑，更相通屬，非為深險絕域之地，車騎安行，無應折磨。

translations.literal: **〔official〕* counties and towns link one to another—not trackless wastes; chariots and horse ride easy—no ground for ‘torture’ talk.
translations.idiomatic: —the text restores ‘official’ counties—lie in chains of settlements, not impassable wastes; wagons roll freely—no excuse of broken terrain.

sentences.zh: 案奐為漢吏，身當武職，駐軍二年，不能平寇，虛欲修文戢戈，招降獷敵，[96]誕辭空說，僭而無征。

translations.literal: Consider: Huan is a Han officer holding military office—two years encamped yet unable to pacify the raiders; emptily talks of culture and sheathed blades, inviting fierce foes to surrender—[96] bombast without proof.
translations.idiomatic: Huan held command two years and achieved nothing; now he mouths Confucian peace to wild enemies—[96] empty boasting.

sentences.zh: 何以言之？

translations.literal: How say this?
translations.idiomatic: Why do I say so?

sentences.zh: 昔先零作寇，趙充國徙令居內，[97]煎當亂邊，馬援遷之三輔，[98]始服終叛，至今為鯁。

translations.literal: Formerly when Xianling raided, Zhao Chongguo moved them inside Lingju; [97] when Jiandang troubled the frontier Ma Yuan shifted them to the Three Adjuncts—[98] they submitted then rebelled and remain a bone in the throat.
translations.idiomatic: Xianling trouble drove Zhao Chongguo to settle them at Lingju; [97] Jiandang risings made Ma Yuan plant them in Sanfu—[98] each time they obeyed then rose; they still choke us.

sentences.zh: [99]故遠識之士，以為深憂。

translations.literal: [99] Far-sighted men count it grave peril.
translations.idiomatic: [99] Wise heads have long warned of this.

sentences.zh: 今傍郡戶口單少，數為羌所創毒，而欲令降徒與之雜居，是猶種枳棘於良田，養虺蛇於室內也。

translations.literal: Now neighboring commanderies have thin populations, repeatedly scarred by Qiang—yet Huan would quarter surrendered migrants among them—like planting thorns in good fields or raising vipers indoors.
translations.idiomatic: Border counties are bled white—yet he would dump surrendered tribes among Han farmers as if sowing brambles in wheat or keeping snakes in the hall.

sentences.zh: 故臣奉大漢之威，建長久之策，欲絕其本根，不使能殖。

translations.literal: Therefore your servant, wielding mighty Han’s awe, frames a lasting policy—to cut the root so it cannot sprout.
translations.idiomatic: I march under Han majesty to rip out the root.

sentences.zh: [100]本規三歲之費，用五十四億，今適開年，所耗未半，而余寇殘燼，將向殄滅。

translations.literal: [100] The original plan counted three years’ cost at five billion four hundred million; only the first year has opened and half remains unspent while remnant embers near extinction.
translations.idiomatic: [100] We budgeted fifty-four hundred million over three years—barely a year in and under half spent—the embers are almost out.

sentences.zh: [101]臣每奉詔書，軍不內御，[102]願卒斯言，一以任臣，臣時量宜，不失權便。」

translations.literal: [101] Each edict says the army need not await interior micromanagement; [102] let that pledge hold—give me sole authority and I will gauge circumstances without missing advantage."
translations.idiomatic: [101] Edicts say field command needs no court micromanagement; [102] honor it—leave execution to me and I will seize every opening."

sentences.zh: 二年，詔遣謁者馮禪說降漢陽散羌。

translations.literal: Second year: an edict sent herald Feng Shan to talk down scattered Qiang in Hanyang.
translations.idiomatic: In year two the court sent Feng Shan to parley with stray Hanyang Qiang.

sentences.zh: 熲以春農，百姓布野，羌雖暫降，而縣官無廩，必當復為盜賊，不如乘虛放兵，埶必殄滅。

translations.literal: Jiong argued spring sowing had peasants spread across the fields; though Qiang might briefly yield, the counties had no granaries—they would turn bandit again—better strike while weak and finish them.
translations.idiomatic: Jiong protested: farmers filled the fields; surrendered bands without state grain would revert to looting—better strike now and extirpate.

sentences.zh: 夏，熲自進營，去羌所屯凡亭山四五十里，遣田晏、夏育將五千人據其山上。

translations.literal: Summer: Jiong advanced his camp forty or fifty li from Fan Mountain where the Qiang camped and sent Tian Yan and Xia Yu with five thousand to seize its heights.
translations.idiomatic: He pushed within forty or fifty li of Fan Mountain and posted Tian Yan and Xia Yu with five thousand atop it.

sentences.zh: 羌悉觿攻之，厲聲問曰：「田晏、夏育在此不？

translations.literal: The Qiang threw their whole force against them and shouted: "Are Tian Yan and Xia Yu here?
translations.idiomatic: The entire Qiang host assailed them, roaring: "Where are Tian Yan and Xia Yu?

sentences.zh: 湟中義從羌悉在何面？

translations.literal: Where stand the Huangzhong volunteer Qiang?
translations.idiomatic: Where are the Huangzhong loyalists?

sentences.zh: 今日欲決死生。

translations.literal: Today we settle life or death.
translations.idiomatic: Today we fight to the death.

sentences.zh: 」軍中恐，晏等勸激兵士，殊死大戰，遂破之。

translations.literal: " The army panicked; Yan and company fired the troops to fight to the uttermost and broke them.
translations.idiomatic: Terror swept the lines until Yan spurred them to a desperate stand—and won.

sentences.zh: 羌觿潰，東奔，復聚射虎谷，分兵守諸谷上下門。

translations.literal: The Qiang host fled east, regathered at Tiger-Shooting Valley, and split detachments to guard upper and lower gorge gates.
translations.idiomatic: Broken, they fled east to Tiger-Shooting Valley and fortified its gates.

sentences.zh: 熲規一舉滅之，不欲復令散走，乃遣千人於西縣結木為柵，廣二十步，長四十里，遮之。

translations.literal: Jiong meant to destroy them in one blow and not let them scatter again; he sent a thousand men west of Xi county to tie timber palisades twenty paces wide and forty li long to block them.
translations.idiomatic: Intent on annihilation, he had a thousand men build a forty-li wooden barrier west of Xi county.

sentences.zh: [103]分遣晏、育等將七千人，銜枚夜上西山，結營穿塹，去虜一里許。

translations.literal: [103] He detached Yan and Yu with seven thousand who muffled gear, climbed the west hill by night, dug trenches a li from the enemy.
translations.idiomatic: [103] Seven thousand under Yan and Yu scaled the west ridge by night, dug in a li off.

sentences.zh: 又遣司馬張愷等將三千人上東山。

translations.literal: He also sent Major Zhang Kai with three thousand up the east hill.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Kai took three thousand up the eastern slope.

sentences.zh: 虜乃覺之，遂攻晏等，分遮汲水道。

translations.literal: The enemy noticed and attacked Yan, splitting forces to cut the water route.
translations.idiomatic: The Qiang woke, struck Yan, and blocked the water parties.

sentences.zh: 熲自率步騎進擊水上，羌□走，因與愷等挾東西山，縱兵擊破之，羌覆敗散。

translations.literal: Jiong personally led foot and horse against them at the river; the Qiang fled; he and Kai pinched east and west hills and broke them—the Qiang collapsed.
translations.idiomatic: Jiong led the ford assault; the Qiang broke; with Kai he squeezed both ridges and shattered them.

sentences.zh: 熲追至谷上下門窮山深谷之中，處處破之，斬其渠帥以下萬九千級，獲牛馬驢騾氈裘廬帳什物，不可勝數。

translations.literal: Jiong chased through upper and lower gates into trackless defiles, crushing pockets everywhere—beheading nineteen thousand rank of chiefs down, capturing oxen, horses, mules, donkeys, felts, tents, and gear beyond count.
translations.idiomatic: He hunted them through the winding gorges—nineteen thousand heads—livestock and baggage uncountable.

sentences.zh: 馮禪等所招降四千人，分置安定、漢陽、隴西三郡，於是東羌悉平。

translations.literal: Four thousand whom Feng Shan had summoned were quartered in Anding, Hanyang, and Longxi—thence the eastern Qiang were wholly pacified.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Shan’s four thousand surrenders went to Anding, Hanyang, and Longxi—the eastern Qiang were finished.

sentences.zh: 凡百八十戰，斬三萬八千六百餘級，獲牛馬羊騾驢駱駝四十二萬七千五百餘頭，費用四十四億，軍士死者四百餘人。

translations.literal: In all one hundred eighty engagements, thirty-eight thousand six hundred-odd heads, four hundred twenty-seven thousand five hundred-odd head of livestock, four billion four hundred million cash spent, and just over four hundred soldiers dead.
translations.idiomatic: One hundred eighty fights, 38,600 heads, 427,500 animals, 4.4 billion cash, four hundred dead.

sentences.zh: 更封新豐縣侯，邑萬戶。

translations.literal: He was re-enfeoffed marquis of Xinfeng county, fief ten thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: His fief became Xinfeng county—ten thousand households.

sentences.zh: 熲行軍仁愛，士卒疾病者，親自瞻省，手為裡創。

translations.literal: Jiong marched with kindness: when men fell ill he visited them himself and dressed their wounds.
translations.idiomatic: He nursed sick soldiers with his own hands.

sentences.zh: 在邊十餘年，未嘗一日蓐寢。

translations.literal: In more than ten years on the frontier he never spent a night on a mattress.
translations.idiomatic: A decade on the march—never a soft bed.

sentences.zh: [104]與將士同苦，故皆樂為死戰。

translations.literal: [104] Sharing hardship with officers and men, they all gladly fought to the death.
translations.idiomatic: [104] Shared misery made every man willing to die.

sentences.zh: 三年春，征還京師，將秦胡步騎五萬餘人，及汗血千里馬，生口萬餘人。

translations.literal: Spring third year he was recalled to the capital leading fifty thousand-plus Qin Hu foot and horse, sweat-blood stallions, and ten thousand-odd captives.
translations.idiomatic: In spring of year three he entered Luoyang with fifty thousand frontier troops, famed horses, and ten thousand captives.

sentences.zh: 詔遣大鴻臚持節慰勞於鎬。

translations.literal: An edict ordered the grand herald with credentials to console him at Hao.
translations.idiomatic: The grand herald met him at Hao with imperial insignia.

sentences.zh: [105]軍至，拜侍中。

translations.literal: [105] When the army arrived he was appointed palace attendant.
translations.idiomatic: [105] At arrival he became palace attendant.

sentences.zh: 轉執金吾河南尹。

translations.literal: Transferred Bearer of the Mace and governor of Henan.
translations.idiomatic: Then Bearer of the Mace and governor of Henan.

sentences.zh: 有盜發馮貴人頤，坐左轉諫議大夫，再遷司隸校尉。

translations.literal: When graverobbers broke into Lady Feng’s tomb he was demoted remonstrance counselor and later promoted colonel director of retainers.
translations.idiomatic: Tomb robbery cost him rank; he rose again to colonel director of retainers.

sentences.zh: 熲曲意宦官，故得保其富貴，遂黨中常侍王甫，枉誅中常侍鄭颯、董騰等，增封四千戶，並前萬四千戶。

translations.literal: Jiong bent to please eunuchs—thus kept wealth and honor—and allied with Regular Attendant Wang Fu, framing fellow attendants Zheng Sa and Dong Teng for execution, gaining four thousand extra households—fourteen thousand with prior fiefs.
translations.idiomatic: He curried eunuchs, backed Wang Fu, framed Zheng Sa and Dong Teng to death, and grew his fief to fourteen thousand households.

sentences.zh: 明年，伐李咸為太尉，其冬病罷，復為司隸校尉。

translations.literal: Next year he replaced Li Xian as grand commandant; that winter illness dismissed him; he returned as colonel director of retainers.
translations.idiomatic: The next year he succeeded Li Xian as grand commandant; illness ended that winter; he became colonel again.

sentences.zh: 數歲，轉穎川太守，徵拜太中大夫。

translations.literal: After several years he turned governor of Yingchuan, then was summoned grand counselor of the palace.
translations.idiomatic: Years later he governed Yingchuan, then grand counselor.

sentences.zh: 光和二年，復代橋玄為太尉。

translations.literal: Second year of Guanghe he again replaced Qiao Xuan as grand commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In Guanghe 2 (179 CE) he succeeded Qiao Xuan as grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 在位月餘，會日食自劾，有司舉奏，詔收印綬，詣廷尉。

translations.literal: Within a month an eclipse moved him to impeach himself; authorities memorialized; an edict stripped his seal and sent him to the Commandant of Justice.
translations.idiomatic: A month in office—eclipse—self-impeachment—seals seized—Commandant of Justice.

sentences.zh: 時司隸校尉陽球奏誅王甫，並及熲，就獄中詰責之，遂飲鴆死，家屬徙邊。

translations.literal: Colonel Director of Retainers Yang Qiu memorialized to execute Wang Fu and implicated Jiong; under jail interrogation he drank poison and died; kin were banished to the frontier.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Qiu’s strike on Wang Fu dragged Jiong in; questioned in prison he swallowed poison; family exiled.

sentences.zh: 後中常侍呂強上疏，追訟熲功，靈帝詔熲妻子還本郡。

translations.literal: Later Regular Attendant Lü Qiang memorialized, appealing Jiong’s merit; Emperor Ling ordered Jiong’s wife and children home to their commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Attendant Lü Qiang later pleaded Jiong’s service; Emperor Ling let his family return.

sentences.zh: 初，熲與皇甫威明、張然明，並知名顯達，京師稱為「涼州三明」云。

translations.literal: At first Jiong with Huangfu Weiming and Zhang Ranming together were famed and eminent; the capital called them the "Three Luminaries of Liangzhou."
translations.idiomatic: Early on Jiong, Huangfu Gui (Weiming), and Zhang Huan (Ranming) rose to fame together—Luoyang dubbed them the Three Luminaries of Liangzhou.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：山西多猛，「三明」儷蹤。

translations.literal: The encomium states: west of the mountains many were fierce; the "Three Luminaries" paired their tracks.
translations.idiomatic: The ode runs: west of the Taihang bred fierce men; the Three Luminaries matched stride for stride.

sentences.zh: [106]戎驂糾結，塵斥河、潼。

translations.literal: [106] Rong chariot-horses tangled; dust filled the Yellow River and Tong Pass.
translations.idiomatic: [106] Barbarian riders swarmed until dust veiled the Yellow River and Tong Pass.

sentences.zh: [107]規、奐審策，亟遏囂凶。

translations.literal: [107] Gui and Huan weighed strategy and urgently blocked raging villains.
translations.idiomatic: [107] Huangfu Gui and Zhang Huan read the field and stemmed the worst violence.

sentences.zh: 文會志比，更相為容。

translations.literal: In civil counsel their aims accorded; each made room for the other.
translations.idiomatic: In council their temperaments harmonized—they yielded to one another.

sentences.zh: 段追兩狄，束馬縣鋒。

translations.literal: Duan pursued the two Di tribes—tied horses, hung spear-points away.
translations.idiomatic: Duan Jiong ran both Di coalitions to ground—bridled horses and sheathed blades.

sentences.zh: 紛紜騰突，谷靜山空。

translations.literal: Turmoil surged and broke; valleys grew quiet, mountains empty.
translations.idiomatic: When the tumult cleared, gorges fell silent and hills stood bare.

sentences.zh: 校勘記

translations.literal: Collation notes
translations.idiomatic: Editorial collation notes

sentences.zh: 二一二九頁七行、規乃上疏求乞自□。

translations.literal: Page 2129 line 7: Gui then submitted a memorial begging [] himself.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: passage reads ' Gui memorialized requesting [lacuna] himself.'

sentences.zh: 按：殿本無「乞」字，王先謙謂無「乞」字是。

translations.literal: Note: the palace edition lacks the character; Wang Xianqian says omission of is correct.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary: the Dian edition drops; Wang Xianqian accepts that reading.

sentences.zh: 二一三一頁一行、流血丹野殿本「丹」作「川」，校補引錢大昭說，謂閩本作「川」。

translations.literal: Page 2131 line 1: 'blood reddened the wilds'—palace edition writes for; Collation Supplement cites Qian Dazhao: Fujian edition reads.
translations.idiomatic: Variant: vs across editions; Fujian has.

sentences.zh: 按：集解引周壽昌說，謂丹野猶赤地也，本書公孫瓚傳有「流血丹水」語，與此同，作「丹」為是。

translations.literal: Note: Collected Explanations cites Zhou Shouchang: means scorched-red earth; Gongsun Zan’s biography has 'blood reddened the waters'—same usage; is correct.
translations.idiomatic: Scholarly note: parallels 'blood reddened the waters' elsewhere—prefer.

sentences.zh: 二一三一頁五行、言國家不妄有□貶進退。

translations.literal: Page 2131 line 5: saying the state does not casually [] promotion and demotion.
translations.idiomatic: Line speaks of the court not lightly [lacuna] advancing or demoting officials.

sentences.zh: 校補謂案文「妄」當作「聞」。

translations.literal: Collation Supplement says according to the text should be.
translations.idiomatic: Editorial emendation: read wen ‘hear’ where the text had wang ‘recklessly’.

sentences.zh: 二一三二頁八行、護羌校尉段熲坐征。

translations.literal: Page 2132 line 8: Colonel Protector of the Qiang Duan Jiong sat summoned.
translations.idiomatic: Witness line: Duan Jiong impeached and recalled.

sentences.zh: 按「段」字原皆斗「□」，逕改正，後如此不悉出校記。

translations.literal: Note: in the archetype the surname was corrupted to lacunae throughout (dou characters); here corrected without repeating each instance.
translations.idiomatic: Scholarly note: missing graphs stood in for Duan’s surname everywhere—normalized silently.

sentences.zh: 二一三二頁一一行、臣生長邠岐。

translations.literal: Page 2132 line 11: your servant was born and grew in Bin and Qi.
translations.idiomatic: Witness: 'born in Bin and Qi.'

sentences.zh: 按：「岐」原斗「歧」，逕據汲本、殿本改正。

translations.literal: Note: Qi was miswritten as qi with the ‘branch’ radical—corrected per Ji and Dian editions.
translations.idiomatic: Glyph correction from ‘fork’ qi to Mount Qi qi.

sentences.zh: 二一三三頁五行、若求猛*

translations.literal: Page 2133 line 5: if one seeks fierce*
translations.idiomatic: Witness: 'if one seeks fierce'

translations.literal: (enemy)
translations.idiomatic: (scribal gloss: enemy)

translations.literal: **〔general〕*.
translations.idiomatic: Restored reading: general (jiang).

sentences.zh: 據汲本、殿本改。

translations.literal: Corrected per Ji and Dian editions.
translations.idiomatic: Emended per Ji and Dian.

sentences.zh: 二一三三頁一三行、沉氐大豪滇昌饑恬等十餘萬口。

translations.literal: Page 2133 line 13: Chenshi great chiefs Dianchang and Jitian and others more than one hundred thousand mouths.
translations.idiomatic: Witness: Dianchang, Jitian, etc., 100,000+.

sentences.zh: 按：集解引惠棟說，謂袁紀作「二十餘萬口」。

translations.literal: Note: Collected Explanations cites Hui Dong: Yuan Ji reads 'more than two hundred thousand mouths.'
translations.idiomatic: Variant: Yuan Ji gives 200,000.

sentences.zh: 二一三四頁二行、急使軍就道。

translations.literal: Page 2134 line 2: urgently ordered the army on the road.
translations.idiomatic: Witness: 'ordered troops onto the road.'

sentences.zh: 按：刊誤謂「軍」上少一字，或「督」或「領」也。

translations.literal: Note: Errata says one character missing above —perhaps or.
translations.idiomatic: Textual note: possible lacuna before (/).

sentences.zh: 二一三五頁一五行、才略兼優。

translations.literal: Page 2135 line 15: talent and strategy both excellent.
translations.idiomatic: Witness: 'talent and strategy both excellent.'

sentences.zh: 按：「兼」原斗「廉」，逕據汲本、殿本改正。

translations.literal: Note: jian ‘combined’ was miswritten as lian ‘incorrupt’—corrected per Ji and Dian editions.
translations.idiomatic: Glyph correction: incorrupt–combined.

sentences.zh: 二一三六頁二行、欲退身避第。

translations.literal: Page 2136 line 2: wished to withdraw body avoiding mansion.
translations.idiomatic: Witness: 'wished to retire from high office.'

sentences.zh: 按：集解引錢大昕說，謂「第」當作「弟」，避弟謂己避位而弟得辟召也，此事見風俗通過譽篇，下文「避第仕途」亦「弟」字之鬥。

translations.literal: Note: Collected Explanations cites Qian Daxin: should be —yielding place so a younger brother might be summoned—recorded in Fengsu tong Guoyu pian; below 'avoid mansion official road' likewise miswrites.
translations.idiomatic: Note: read for —step aside for a sibling’s appointment per Fengsu tong.

sentences.zh: 二一三六頁七行、時人以為規賢。

translations.literal: Page 2136 line 7: people of the time considered Gui worthy.
translations.idiomatic: Witness: 'people deemed Gui worthy.'

sentences.zh: 按：刊誤謂案文當作「以規為賢」。

translations.literal: Note: Errata says text should read 'deemed Gui worthy.'
translations.idiomatic: Preferred wording:.

sentences.zh: 二一三七頁一行、誅鄧萬。

translations.literal: Page 2137 line 1: executed Deng Wan.
translations.idiomatic: Witness: executed Deng Wan.

sentences.zh: 按：校補謂鄧萬即鄧萬世，章懷避唐諱，省去一「世」字。

translations.literal: Note: Collation Supplement says Deng Wan is Deng Wanshi—Zhang Huai avoided Tang taboo by dropping one.
translations.idiomatic: Name note: Deng Wan = Deng Wanshi (taboo truncation).

sentences.zh: 二一三八頁一行、敦煌*

translations.literal: Page 2138 line 1: Dunhuang*
translations.idiomatic: Witness: Dunhuang—

translations.literal: (wine)
translations.idiomatic: (corrupt gloss: wine)

sentences.zh: **〔淵〕*泉人也。

translations.literal: **〔Yuan〕*quan man.
translations.idiomatic: Read Yuanquan as the county name.

sentences.zh: 按：集解引錢大昕說，謂酒泉郡名，非縣名，當作「淵泉」。

translations.literal: Note: Collected Explanations cites Qian Daxin: Jiuquan is a commandery name, not a county—it should be Yuanquan.
translations.idiomatic: Geographic fix: Yuanquan county, not Jiuquan.

sentences.zh: 漢志敦煌郡有淵泉縣，晉志作「深泉」，蓋避唐諱。

translations.literal: Han treatise: Dunhuang commandery had Yuanquan county; Jin treatise writes Shenquan—Tang taboo.
translations.idiomatic: Han zhi: Yuanquan; Jin zhi: Shenquan (taboo).

sentences.zh: 章懷本亦當作「深」，後人妄改為「酒」耳。

translations.literal: Zhang Huai’s edition originally should read Shen; later men wrongly changed it to wine.
translations.idiomatic: Post-Zhang Huai corruption →.

sentences.zh: 胡注通鑒雲奐敦煌淵泉人，胡所見本尚未鬥也。

translations.literal: Hu Sanxing’s Zizhi tongjian commentary says Huan was a Yuanquan man of Dunhuang—Hu’s copy was not yet corrupted.
translations.idiomatic: Hu Sanxing still read.

sentences.zh: 今據改。

translations.literal: Now corrected accordingly.
translations.idiomatic: Emended.

sentences.zh: 注同。

translations.literal: Same for commentary.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary likewise.

sentences.zh: 二一三八頁四行、*

translations.literal: Page 2138 line 4: *
translations.idiomatic: Witness fragment—

translations.literal: (yang)
translations.idiomatic: (scribal: yang)

sentences.zh: **〔瓜〕*州晉昌縣。

translations.literal: **〔Gua〕*zhou Jinchang county.
translations.idiomatic: Restore Gua prefecture and Jinchang county.

sentences.zh: 汲本、殿本「陽」作「永」。

translations.literal: Ji and Dian editions write yong where others read yang.
translations.idiomatic: Variant graphs yong versus yang in editions.

sentences.zh: 按：刊誤謂「永」當作「瓜」。

translations.literal: Note: Errata says yong should be emended to gua.
translations.idiomatic: Editorial note: read gua for yong.

sentences.zh: 集解引錢大昕說，謂閩本「永」作「陽」，考唐書地理志，晉昌縣屬瓜州，永陽二字俱誤。

translations.literal: Collected Explanations cites Qian Daxin: Fujian editions swap yong for yang; Tang geography lists Jinchang under Gua prefecture—both graphs are wrong.
translations.idiomatic: Qian Daxin argues Jinchang belongs under Gua—the readings yong and yang both fail.

sentences.zh: 今據改。

translations.literal: Now corrected accordingly.
translations.idiomatic: Emended.

sentences.zh: 二一三八頁五行、時牟卿受書於張堪。

translations.literal: Page 2138 line 5: at the time Mou Qing received the Documents from Zhang Kan.
translations.idiomatic: Witness: Mou Qing studied under Zhang Kan.

sentences.zh: 按：集解引洪亮吉說，謂「張」字應作「周」字。

translations.literal: Note: Collected Explanations cites Hong Liangji: should be.
translations.idiomatic: Hong Liangji: emend →.

sentences.zh: 二一三八頁一四行、金*

translations.literal: Page 2138 line 14: gold*
translations.idiomatic: Witness: gold—

translations.literal: (food)
translations.idiomatic: (corrupt gloss)

translations.literal: **〔silver〕* vessel name.
translations.idiomatic: Read silver vessel where the gloss miswrites ‘food’.

sentences.zh: 集解引洪頤粻說，謂中山經郭注，鐻，金銀器之名。

translations.literal: Collected Explanations cites Hong Yizong: Guo’s Zhongshan jing commentary defines ju as a precious-metal vessel.
translations.idiomatic: Hong explains ju as a silver-or-gold ritual vessel.

sentences.zh: 李注「食」當是「銀」字之鬥。

translations.literal: Li Shan’s gloss treats shi ‘food’ as a corrupted graph for yin ‘silver’.
translations.idiomatic: Li Shan emends the gloss from ‘food’ to ‘silver’.

sentences.zh: 今據改。

translations.literal: Now corrected accordingly.
translations.idiomatic: Emended.

sentences.zh: 二一四一頁八行、天乃雷雨以風。

translations.literal: Page 2141 line 8: heaven then sent thunder-rain with wind.
translations.idiomatic: Witness: Zhougong omens—storm.

sentences.zh: 按：汲本、殿本「雨」作「電」。

translations.literal: Note: Ji and Dian editions read lightning for rain.
translations.idiomatic: Ji/Dian variant lightning versus rain.

sentences.zh: 二一四二頁一三行、穰穰滿家。

translations.literal: Page 2142 line 13: abundance filling the house.
translations.idiomatic: Witness: 'granaries overflowing.'

sentences.zh: 按：「穰穰」原斗「禳禳」，逕據汲本、殿本改正。

translations.literal: Note: rangrang ‘exorcism’ was miswritten for rangrang ‘bumper harvest’—corrected per Ji and Dian editions.
translations.idiomatic: Glyph correction between homophonous harvest and ritual graphs.

sentences.zh: 二一四三頁五行、乃以五百金買其首以報。

translations.literal: Page 2143 line 5: then used five hundred gold to buy its head in return.
translations.idiomatic: Witness: paid five hundred gold for the head.

sentences.zh: 按：校補引柳從辰說，謂今新序「首」作「骨」。

translations.literal: Note: Collation Supplement cites Liu Chenchen: later Xinshu editions read bone where our text reads head.
translations.idiomatic: Modern Xinshu parallels prefer bone over head.

sentences.zh: 案北史隱逸傳崔賾荅豫章王書「燕求馬首，□養□鳴」，知古本原有作「首」者。

translations.literal: Beishi recluse biography preserves Cui Yin’s reply—‘Yan sought horses’ heads…’—proving older witnesses used head.
translations.idiomatic: Northern History parallels confirm head appears in early witnesses.

sentences.zh: 南史鄭鮮之傳「燕昭市骨而駿足至」，則仍作「骨」。

translations.literal: Nanshi biography of Zheng Xianzhi says Yan Zhao bought bones and swift horses arrived—still using bone.
translations.idiomatic: Southern History sticks with bone for the parable.

sentences.zh: 且孔融與魏武論盛孝章書已云「燕君市駿馬之骨」，是作「骨」亦由來已久。

translations.literal: Kong Rong’s letter to Cao Cao already says Yan’s lord bought prized horse bones—bone therefore long canonical.
translations.idiomatic: Kong Rong’s wording shows bone had literary sanction early.

sentences.zh: 疑新序自有南北本之別，唐起北方，章懷所據蓋是北本。

translations.literal: Suspect Xinshu had northern and southern recensions; Tang rose in the north—Zhang Huai’s source was probably northern.
translations.idiomatic: Text-family hypothesis for Xinshu variants.

sentences.zh: 二一四三頁一六行、奢非晉文。

translations.literal: Page 2143 line 16: extravagance is not Duke Wen of Jin.
translations.idiomatic: Witness line reading against extravagant Jin-wen burial parallels.

sentences.zh: 按：集解引惠棟說，謂「晉」續漢書作「桓」，據注引齊桓公事，疑本書亦元是「桓」字。

translations.literal: Collected Explanations cites Hui Dong: Xu Hanshu writes Huan for Jin—given the Qi Duke Huan anecdote in commentary, the archetype likely read Huan.
translations.idiomatic: Hui Dong prefers Duke Huan of Qi over Duke Wen of Jin.

sentences.zh: 二一四四頁八行、王愔文志。

translations.literal: Page 2144 line 8: Wang Kai Wen zhi.
translations.idiomatic: Witness: Wang Kai’s Wen zhi.

sentences.zh: 按：殿本「文志」作「文字志」。

translations.literal: Note: Dian edition expands Wen zhi to Wen zi zhi.
translations.idiomatic: Palace edition adds zi to the title.

sentences.zh: 二一四五頁六行、所在*〔有〕*能政。

translations.literal: Page 2145 line 6: wherever *[have]* capable governance.
translations.idiomatic: Witness: lacuna filled with.

sentences.zh: 據刊誤補。

translations.literal: Supplied per Errata.
translations.idiomatic: Errata supplement.

sentences.zh: 二一四五頁八行、*〔會〕*宗字子松。

translations.literal: Page 2145 line 8: *[Hui]*zong courtesy Zisong.
translations.idiomatic: Supply for lacuna.

sentences.zh: 據殿本補。

translations.literal: Supplied per Dian edition.
translations.idiomatic: Dian supplement.

sentences.zh: 二一四六頁一行、司徒尹*

translations.literal: Page 2146 line 1: Minister of Education Yin*
translations.idiomatic: Witness: Yin—

translations.literal: (litigation)
translations.idiomatic: (corrupt:)

sentences.zh: **〔頌〕*薦熲通。

translations.literal: **〔Song〕* recommended Jiong comprehensively.
translations.idiomatic: Restore Yin Song’s personal name.

sentences.zh: 鑒胡注謂桓帝紀「訟」作「頌」，作「頌」為是。

translations.literal: Hu commentary notes Emperor Huan annals write for — is correct.
translations.idiomatic: Hu Sanxing: correct.

sentences.zh: 今據改。

translations.literal: Now corrected accordingly.
translations.idiomatic: Emended.

sentences.zh: 注同。

translations.literal: Same for commentary.
translations.idiomatic: Commentary likewise.

sentences.zh: 二一四六頁一〇行、首虜五千餘人。

translations.literal: Page 2146 line 10: heads and captives more than five thousand persons.
translations.idiomatic: Witness line tallying five thousand heads or captives.

sentences.zh: 按：「千」原斗「十」，逕據汲本、殿本改正。

translations.literal: Note: thousand was miswritten as ten—corrected per Ji and Dian editions.
translations.idiomatic: Numeral correction ten to thousand.

sentences.zh: 二一四六頁一一行、燒當種九十餘口詣熲降。

translations.literal: Page 2146 line 11: Shaodang kind more than ninety mouths went to Jiong to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: Witness: ninety-odd Shaodang households.

sentences.zh: 按：刊誤謂燒當一種不止九十餘口，其種中九十口降亦不足記，「十」當作「千」。

translations.literal: Note: Errata argues Shaodang outnumbers ninety mouths total—ninety households surrendering would be trivial—ten should read thousand.
translations.idiomatic: Editorial suggestion emends tens to thousands.

sentences.zh: 二一四八頁八行、徒更招降。

translations.literal: Page 2148 line 8: only further summon surrender.
translations.idiomatic: Witness line discussing redundant summons to surrender.

sentences.zh: 按：「徒」字疑鬥，通鑒作「欲」。

translations.literal: Note: tu ‘only’ may be corrupt—Zizhi tongjian reads yu ‘wish’.
translations.idiomatic: Zizhi tongjian substitutes wish for only.

sentences.zh: 二一四九頁五行、乃令軍中張鏃利刃刊誤謂案文鏃非可張，未知何字。

translations.literal: Page 2149 line 5: ordered camp to display arrowheads sharp blades—Errata says arrowheads cannot be 'displayed'—character uncertain.
translations.idiomatic: Textual crux on.

sentences.zh: 按：殿本考證謂通鑒「張」作「長」。

translations.literal: Note: Dian textual commentary records Zizhi tongjian writing ‘draw out’ for ‘spread’.
translations.idiomatic: Palace investigation cites Zizhi tongjian variant.

sentences.zh: 二一五〇頁六行、段熲*

translations.literal: Page 2150 line 6: Duan Jiong*
translations.idiomatic: Witness: Duan Jiong—

translations.literal: (said)
translations.idiomatic: (corrupt gloss)

translations.literal: *biography*〔said〕*.
translations.idiomatic: Restore biography citation marker.

sentences.zh: 據汲本改。

translations.literal: Corrected per Ji edition.
translations.idiomatic: Ji edition fix.

sentences.zh: 二一五一頁六行、故*

translations.literal: Page 2151 line 6: former*
translations.idiomatic: Witness: former—

translations.literal: (palace)
translations.idiomatic: (corrupt: palace)

sentences.zh: **〔官〕*縣邑更相通屬。

translations.literal: **〔official〕* counties and towns mutually linked.
translations.idiomatic: Restore official counties linking together.

sentences.zh: 據汲本改。

translations.literal: Corrected per Ji edition.
translations.idiomatic: Ji edition fix.

sentences.zh: 按：刊誤謂案文「宮」當作「官」，舊屯田營壁皆是故官也。

translations.literal: Note: Errata argues palace should read official—former military colonies lined earlier county seats.
translations.idiomatic: Editor reads official offices where editions wrongly wrote palace.

sentences.zh: 懸猶停也。

translations.literal: Commentator gloss: xuan here means halt.
translations.idiomatic: Gloss: the graph denotes stopping.

sentences.zh: 平人，齊人也。

translations.literal: Ping Ren was a man of Qi.
translations.idiomatic: The commentator identifies Ping Ren as a native of Qi.

sentences.zh: 振，整；

translations.literal: Zhen means to array; to set in order.
translations.idiomatic: Gloss: zhen, to draw up or regulate formation.

sentences.zh: 旅，觿也。

translations.literal: Lu means the host; the assembled force; also glossed as army.
translations.idiomatic: Lu denotes the marching host—the soldiery as a body.

sentences.zh: 穀梁傳曰「出曰治兵，入曰振旅」也。

translations.literal: The Guliang Commentary says: "Going forth is called drilling troops; returning is called marshaling the host."
translations.idiomatic: From the Guliang: "outbound maneuvers are zhibing; inbound review is zhenlu."

sentences.zh: 兩營謂馬賢及趙沖等。

translations.literal: The two camps refer to Ma Xian and Zhao Chong and the rest.
translations.idiomatic: Both encampments: Ma Xian’s and Zhao Chong’s commands.

sentences.zh: 二郡，安定、隴西也。

translations.literal: The two commanderies are Anding and Longxi.
translations.idiomatic: Anding and Longxi—the paired frontier jurisdictions.

sentences.zh: 邁，往也。

translations.literal: Mai means to go; to proceed.
translations.idiomatic: Mai: to march or advance.

sentences.zh: 近習，諸佞幸親近小人也。

translations.literal: Near attendants means the petty flatterers who crowd close to the ruler.
translations.idiomatic: Intimates at court—sycophants who nestle beside the throne.

sentences.zh: 禮記曰：「雖有貴戚近習。」

translations.literal: The Record of Rites says: "Though there be noble in-laws and near attendants."
translations.idiomatic: A line from the Liji on kin and palace favorites.

sentences.zh: 左傳曰「人患王之無厭也，故從亂如歸」也。

translations.literal: The Zuo Commentary says: "The people feared the king’s insatiability, so they followed rebellion as if going home."
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo gloss: popular dread of royal greed turns revolt into a refuge.

sentences.zh: 先後謂進退也。

translations.literal: Xianhou means advance and retreat.
translations.idiomatic: Order of movement—forward motion and pullback.

sentences.zh: 言國家不妄有□貶進退，而權幸之徒反為禍福也。

translations.literal: It says the state does not rashly grant promotions or demotions without cause, yet men of power and favor invert fortune and disaster.
translations.idiomatic: The point: personnel policy should stay steady, while favorites twist outcomes.

sentences.zh: 詩大雅曰：「旱魃為虐，如惔如焚。

translations.literal: The Greater Odes says: "The drought demon works havoc, blistering like fire."
translations.idiomatic: A line from the Mao Shi on the ba scourge—drought like flame.

sentences.zh: 」魃，旱神也。

translations.literal: Ba is the spirit of drought.
translations.idiomatic: Ba: the drought fiend of folklore.

sentences.zh: 無狀者，謂無善狀。

translations.literal: Those without good record means lacking worthy conduct.
translations.idiomatic: Wu zhuang—no credible merit on the books.

sentences.zh: 梁商女為順帝后，後女弟又為桓帝后。

translations.literal: Liang Shang’s daughter became Empress to Emperor Shun; later another younger sister became Empress to Emperor Huan.
translations.idiomatic: Two Liang daughters crowned—ties to Shun and Huan.

sentences.zh: 冀即商子，故曰代姻也。

translations.literal: Ji was Shang’s son; hence it speaks of successive marriage alliances.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Ji inherits those marital bonds with the throne.

sentences.zh: 可猶宜也。

translations.literal: Ke means ought; fittingly.
translations.idiomatic: Ke: appropriately; it should be so.

sentences.zh: 家語孔子曰：「夫君者舟也，人者水也。

translations.literal: The Family Sayings records Confucius: "The lord is the boat; the people are the water."
translations.idiomatic: The Jia Yu image casts the throne as the hull and the people as the tide.

sentences.zh: 水可載舟，亦以覆舟。

translations.literal: Water can float the boat and can also overturn it.
translations.idiomatic: The same element bears the realm or swamps it.

sentences.zh: 君以此思危，則可知也。」

translations.literal: If the ruler ponders this peril, he may know how to rule.
translations.idiomatic: Closing counsel from the passage on sober rule.

sentences.zh: 熲擊羌，坐為涼州刺史郭閎留兵不進下獄。

translations.literal: When striking the Qiang, Jiong was impeached and imprisoned because Liangzhou Inspector Guo Hong held troops back and would not advance.
translations.idiomatic: Jiong’s Qiang campaign ended in jail—Guo Hong stalled the army.

sentences.zh: 說文曰：「塢，小障也。

translations.literal: The Shuowen says: "Wu, a small barrier."
translations.idiomatic: Shuowen gloss on wu—miniature rampart.

sentences.zh: 一曰庳城也。

translations.literal: Another reading calls it a low wall.
translations.idiomatic: Alternate gloss: a squat enclosing wall.

sentences.zh: 」音烏古反。

translations.literal: Fanqie reading: wu-gu fan.
translations.idiomatic: Phonetic fanqie: wu plus gu.

sentences.zh: 郡將，郡守也。

translations.literal: Junjiang means the commandery governor.
translations.idiomatic: County chief—the commanding guardian of the commandery.

sentences.zh: 鳥鼠，山名，在今渭州西，即先零羌寇鈔處也。

translations.literal: Niaoshu is a mountain name, west of modern Weizhou, where the Xianling Qiang raided.
translations.idiomatic: Bird-Rat Mountain—scene of Xianling sorties west of Weizhou.

sentences.zh: 東岱謂泰山，叔孫無忌反處也。

translations.literal: Eastern Dai means Mount Tai, where Sun Wuji rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: Taishan anchor—Sun Wuji’s rising.

sentences.zh: 皆由郡守不加綏撫，致使反叛，其疾同也。

translations.literal: All stemmed from commandery governors failing to soothe and comfort the people, provoking rebellion—the ailment was the same everywhere.
translations.idiomatic: Shared pathology: neglectful magistrates seed revolt.

sentences.zh: 吳起，魏將也。

translations.literal: Wu Qi was a general of Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Wu Qi—the Wei strategist.

sentences.zh: 孫武，吳將也。

translations.literal: Sun Wu was a general of Wu.
translations.idiomatic: Sun Wu—Wu’s classical tactician.

sentences.zh: 言若求猛*

translations.literal: The text says: If one seeks fierce—
translations.idiomatic: The argument opens: craving sheer ferocity—

translations.literal: (enemy)
translations.idiomatic: Marginal gloss (enemy).

sentences.zh: **〔將〕*，不如撫以青平之政；

translations.literal: ** marginal gloss general *—it is better to soothe them with pure, even-handed governance;
translations.idiomatic: Rather than hunting a fierce enemy general, better soothe them with calm, even-handed rule;

sentences.zh: 明習兵書，不如郡守奉法，使之無反也。

translations.literal: being versed in military manuals is inferior to a commandery governor who upholds the law so they never rebel.
translations.idiomatic: Tomes of war matter less than a lawful governor who keeps peace.

sentences.zh: 戚，憂也。

translations.literal: Qi means sorrow; worry.
translations.idiomatic: Qi: anxious care.

sentences.zh: 前變謂羌反。

translations.literal: Prior turmoil means the Qiang rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier crisis—the Qiang uprising.

sentences.zh: 以文簿虛降，非真心也。

translations.literal: Surrender on ledgers alone is not sincere intent.
translations.idiomatic: Paper capitulation—no true submission.

sentences.zh: 蠢，動也。

translations.literal: Chun means to stir; to move.
translations.idiomatic: Chun: restless motion.

sentences.zh: 戾，乖也。

translations.literal: Li means perverse; refractory.
translations.idiomatic: Li: twisted, contrary.

sentences.zh: 縣名，屬安定郡，其故城在今原州平源縣南也。

translations.literal: A county subordinate to Anding commandery; its old walled town lay south of Pingyuan County in Yuanzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Anding county seat—ruins south of Pingyuan in Yuanzhou.

sentences.zh: 就猶上也。

translations.literal: Jiu means to submit upward; to present.
translations.idiomatic: Jiu: to lay before superiors.

sentences.zh: 訪，問也。

translations.literal: Fang means to inquire.
translations.idiomatic: Fang: to examine by questioning.

sentences.zh: 規言羌種既服，臣即移書軍營及郡，勘問誅殺並納受多少之數目也。

translations.literal: Gui states that once the Qiang submitted, he immediately sent letters to camps and commanderies auditing how many were killed and how many enrolled.
translations.idiomatic: Gui’s audit—dispatch tallies of slain and enrolled across camps.

sentences.zh: 詩小雅曰：「密勿從事，不敢告勞。

translations.literal: The Minor Odes says: "Diligently serving, I dare not speak of my toil."
translations.idiomatic: A Xiao Ya line on thankless labor.

sentences.zh: 無罪無辜，讒口□□。」

translations.literal: Without crime or guilt, slanderous mouths ….
translations.idiomatic: The innocent maligned—lacunae mar the verse.

sentences.zh: 先事謂前輩敗將也。

translations.literal: Prior affairs means the earlier defeated generals.
translations.idiomatic: Those earlier routs—the veteran failures.

sentences.zh: 謝猶讎也。

translations.literal: Xie means to repay; it is like enmity.
translations.idiomatic: Xie: settling scores like feud.

sentences.zh: 元帝賜呼韓邪單于待詔掖庭王嬙為閼氏也。

translations.literal: Emperor Yuan granted Lady Wang Zhaojun of the Yeting awaiting-edicts to Chanyu Huhanye as Khatun.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan’s marriage diplomacy—Zhaojun to Huhanye.

sentences.zh: 武帝以江都王建女細君妻烏孫王昆莫為夫人也。

translations.literal: Emperor Wu married Princess Xijun, daughter of Prince Jian of Jiangdu, to Kunmo king of Wusun as consort.
translations.idiomatic: Wu’s western alliance—Xijun to the Wusun ruler.

sentences.zh: 言覆軍之將，旋師之日，多載珍寶，封印完全，便入權門。

translations.literal: It says defeated generals, when they wheel the army home, often load treasures with seals intact straight into powerful houses.
translations.idiomatic: Routed commanders freight loot to patron villas—wax seals unbroken.

sentences.zh: 左傳曰「鹿死不擇音，鋌而走險，急何能擇」也。

translations.literal: The Zuo Commentary says: "When the deer dies it does not choose its cry; metal flees danger—when pressed, how can one choose?"
translations.idiomatic: The Zuo maxim: desperation knows no scruple.

sentences.zh: 《漢官儀》曰：左校署屬將作大匠也。

translations.literal: The Han Official Regulations says: The Left Workshop belongs to the Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Per the Han Official Regulations, the Left Workshop falls under the chief builder.

sentences.zh: 言欲歸第避仕宦之塗也。

translations.literal: It means wishing to retire home and avoid the track of office.
translations.idiomatic: Yearning to quit the bureaucratic road for private life.

sentences.zh: 謂誅梁冀，誅鄧萬、鄧會，誅李膺等黨事也。

translations.literal: It refers to executing Liang Ji; executing Deng Wan and Deng Hui; executing Li Ying and the rest in the partisan affair.
